name: Sif
description: Sif is an Asynjur, a goddess in Norse mythology associated with earth, fertility, grain, and harvest. She is the wife of Thor, the god of thunder, lightning, storms, oak trees, strength, and the protection of humankind. Sif is primarily known for her beautiful golden hair, which was said to be as radiant as fields of ripe wheat. This hair was not naturally grown, however. In a tale of mischief, Loki, in one of his many acts of trickery, cut off Sif's hair. An enraged Thor threatened Loki with violence, forcing him to have the dwarves, the Sons of Ivaldi, forge a new head of hair for Sif made of pure gold that would grow like normal hair once placed on her head. This incident highlights not only Loki's mischievous nature but also the importance of Sif's hair as a symbol of fertility and prosperity. While Sif's role in many myths is relatively minor, she is often mentioned as the mother of Þrúðr by Thor and Ullr by an unknown father. She represents the bounty of the earth and the vital role of women in ensuring the prosperity of the community through agriculture. Sif's presence adds to the richness and complexity of the Norse pantheon, symbolizing the interconnectedness of gods, humans, and the natural world.
subtitle: Goddess of Earth, Fertility, and Harvest

keyMyths.title: Loki Cuts Sif's Hair
keyMyths.description: Loki, in a mischievous act, cuts off Sif's golden hair. Thor forces Loki to replace it with hair made of gold by the dwarves.

keyMyths.title: Mother of Þrúðr
keyMyths.description: Sif is the mother of Þrúðr, a Valkyrie, by Thor. Þrúðr is associated with strength and might.

keyMyths.title: Mother of Ullr
keyMyths.description: Sif is the mother of Ullr, a god of hunting, archery, skiing, and single combat. His father is unknown.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, including mentions of Sif.
